evals.prompt: Using the supplied Godot project snapshot, provide the smallest coherent player-dash patch. Reuse its Input Map, CharacterBody2D collision movement, Timer, and HUD contract. Explain the exact running-game checks, but do not claim they ran in this text-only harness.
evals.expected_output: Uses the existing Godot 4 dash action, Player-owned DashCooldown Timer, move_and_slide collision path, HUD method, and always-processing PauseController; leaves Player and Timer pausable so dash and cooldown freeze while paused, and gives honest runtime checks.
assertions: The patch is compatible with Godot 4 and reuses the existing dash action, Player-owned Timer, move_and_slide collision path, and HUD set_dash_cooldown contract
The patch preserves the always-processing PauseController and pausable Player/Timer contract, and wall collision, one-dash-per-cooldown, HUD progress, plus text-only verification limits are explicit

evals.prompt: Our Godot game loads dialogue JSON in the editor but fails after a Windows export. Diagnose the packaging issue and give the smallest fix.
evals.expected_output: Checks resource versus non-resource export handling, path casing, export presets, and confirms the exported build rather than assuming editor behavior is enough.
assertions: The diagnosis distinguishes editor behavior from exported-build behavior
Export configuration and runtime asset inclusion are inspected

evals.prompt: Implement three save slots and a load screen in an existing Godot project without breaking the current scene transitions.
evals.expected_output: Maps scene ownership, persistent data, resource paths, and transitions before proposing the smallest save/load and UI changes.
assertions: Persistent state and scene-transition contracts are examined
The proposed verification covers save, reload, and missing or corrupt slot behavior

evals.prompt: Our Godot C# combat scene hitches whenever enemies spawn. Profile the project, identify the smallest likely source, and verify the fix without guessing at the engine version.
evals.expected_output: Confirms the Godot version and C# setup, uses profiler evidence, changes the responsible path, and reruns the same measurement.
assertions: The diagnosis is based on profiler or reproduction evidence rather than a generic optimization list
Version and language compatibility are checked before engine-specific advice

evals.prompt: Add keyboard and controller rebinding to our Godot 4 settings screen while preserving the existing Input Map and pause behavior.
evals.expected_output: Uses named input actions, checks device and pause behavior, and verifies the settings flow in the running game.
assertions: Bindings are changed through the Input Map rather than scattered raw events
The verification covers controller, keyboard, and pause-state behavior

evals.prompt: Plan how to implement our Godot RPG graphic style and story, connect combat, AI, level, quest, inventory, dialogue, UI, animation, audio, save systems, and prove them in one production-ready vertical slice.
evals.expected_output: Creates measurable visual and story contracts, maps system ownership and persistence, chooses a representative slice, defines explicit connection rules, and climbs the test ladder through target export and human playtest.
assertions: The plan maps authored definitions, runtime owners, persisted state, inputs, outputs, failures, and tests for every connected system
Graphic style, story, save continuity, performance, accessibility, and export are proven together in a bounded vertical slice

evals.prompt: Our roguelite starts to feel repetitive after twenty minutes. Redesign the run loop, rewards, and risk choices before we build anything.
evals.expected_output: Should prefer gameplay-consultant because the primary task is player experience and game design, not Godot implementation.

evals.prompt: Fix our Unity Addressables loading race and add a regression test.
evals.expected_output: Should not require this skill because the request is Unity-specific rather than a Godot project task.

evals.prompt: Our roguelite reward choices have become predictable. Redesign the risk, progression, and player decision before implementation.
evals.expected_output: Should prefer gameplay-consultant because the primary work is game-design diagnosis and playtesting, not Godot implementation.
